long: 1 · Manual craft (do it well by hand)

themes.title: Build on the master
notes: slide masters, layouts, placeholders — the deck's skeleton
rungs: Plan the deck's structure: which layouts, in what order
Create the section skeleton from a prompt or a document
Build the deck skeleton from an outline file, on the right layouts

themes.title: Arrange & align
notes: guides, distribute, grouping — the formatting demand
rungs: Diagnose a slide you describe: what makes it look untidy
Apply Designer suggestions — then audit what it changed
Batch-fix alignment and spacing across a deck by script

themes.title: Charts & visuals
notes: data onto slides; the Excel seam (paste-link, not screenshot)
rungs: Pick the right visual for the point you describe
Create a slide visualising this data, in the deck
Generate the charts into the deck from the data files

themes.title: Reuse & templates
notes: the corporate template; theme colours/fonts; reusable slides
rungs: Turn a deck you describe into a reusable template checklist
Create a new deck from the organisation's template
Build the organisation template itself from a brand spec

themes.title: Present & share
notes: presenter view, rehearse, export — the last mile
rungs: Build the pre-presentation checklist for the room you describe
Summarise this deck for the follow-up email
Build the export pack (PDF, handouts, notes pages) by script

lessons.title: Masters & layouts: fix it once, fix it everywhere
Align, distribute, group: the tidy slide
Whitespace & hierarchy: less on the slide
Excel to slide: paste-link, never screenshot
Working the corporate template (not against it)
Export & share: PDF, handouts, the deck that travels
